metadata.archetype_justification: The service maintains mutable in-memory state (service registry) and exposes both read (instance lookup, registry fetch) and write (registration, deregistration, heartbeat, status update) endpoints via REST API. Peer-to-peer replication propagates state mutations across instances.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: All compute designed for raw EC2 deployment (WAR on servlet container with EIP/ENI binding)
top_gaps.impact: Requires manual server management, patching, capacity planning; blocks containerization and serverless adoption

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code
top_gaps.gap: No IaC exists — all infrastructure manually provisioned
top_gaps.impact: Non-reproducible environments, manual error-prone deployments, no disaster recovery automation

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC/subnet/security group definitions in repo
top_gaps.impact: No documented network isolation; services potentially directly exposed

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, DAST, or dependency scanning in CI/CD pipeline
top_gaps.impact: Vulnerabilities in dependencies (AWS SDK 1.11.277, Jersey 1.x) and code reach production undetected

top_gaps.title: Async vs Sync Communication
top_gaps.gap: All inter-node communication is synchronous HTTP with no async patterns for state propagation
top_gaps.impact: Cascading failures during peer replication, tight coupling between Eureka nodes, no resilience to slow peers

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 2) — GitHub Actions workflows for build, publish, and snapshot are functional
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers builds, checks CI status, monitors publish workflows, and manages release tagging
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Add GitHub API access configuration for the agent to interact with Actions workflows programmatically

quick_agent_wins.name: Integration Test Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Integration tests exist and run in CI (OPS-Q6 = 3) — EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest, MockServer, and WireMock tests are established
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that monitors test results, identifies flaky tests, suggests test improvements, and correlates failures with code changes
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose test result artifacts from CI in a structured format (JUnit XML reports)

findings.title: Managed Compute: All compute on raw EC2
findings.description: Eureka is designed for deployment as a WAR file on EC2 instances. The codebase contains deep EC2 integration: EIPManager.java manages Elastic IP binding, ElasticNetworkInterfaceBinder.java handles ENI attachment, Route53Binder.java manages DNS records, and AmazonInfo.java reads EC2 instance metadata. No managed compute (ECS, EKS, Lambda, Fargate) is used or supported.
findings.gap: All compute is on raw EC2 with no managed container orchestration or serverless. The WAR deployment model and EC2-specific identity code prevent adoption of managed compute without refactoring.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the Eureka server with a Dockerfile wrapping the WAR in a Tomcat/Jetty base image. Deploy on Amazon EKS (preferred). Abstract EC2-specific identity code behind an interface that supports both EC2 and Kubernetes service discovery.

findings.title: Workflow Orchestration: All workflows hardcoded
findings.description: Multi-step workflows exist (peer replication, EIP/ENI lifecycle management, self-preservation transitions) but all orchestration logic is hardcoded in application code with no dedicated workflow service.
findings.gap: No orchestration — all workflow logic (peer replication, EIP lifecycle, cluster formation) is hardcoded in application code.
findings.recommendation: For containerized deployment on EKS, consider AWS Step Functions for EIP/DNS lifecycle operations during scaling events. Peer replication orchestration could benefit from EventBridge for event-driven cluster membership changes.

findings.title: Async Messaging: No messaging for state propagation
findings.description: No managed messaging or streaming infrastructure exists. All state propagation between Eureka nodes uses synchronous HTTP-based replication. The in-process netflix-eventbus (0.3.0) is used for local event dispatch only.
findings.gap: No messaging where state changes cross service boundaries — peer replication is entirely synchronous HTTP between Eureka nodes, creating tight coupling and cascading failure risk.
findings.recommendation: Consider Amazon EventBridge (preferred) for decoupled peer notification of registry changes. SQS could buffer replication events to peers, preventing cascading failures during node degradation.

findings.title: Network Security: No VPC configuration defined
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation configuration exists in this repository. The application is designed for EC2 deployment but no networking infrastructure is defined.
findings.gap: Services deployed without documented network isolation. No VPC configuration, security groups, or network segmentation visible.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC infrastructure in IaC (CDK or Terraform) with private subnets for Eureka nodes. Implement least-privilege security groups allowing only intra-cluster communication and client traffic on the Eureka port.

findings.title: API Entry Point: No gateway or load balancer
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront serves as the entry point. Eureka nodes are accessed directly via their EIP or DNS record. The Route53Binder registers individual instance DNS records, and clients connect directly.
findings.gap: Services exposed directly with no gateway or load balancer. No throttling, authentication, or request validation at the entry point.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an Application Load Balancer or API Gateway (preferred) in front of Eureka nodes for health checking, traffic distribution, and TLS termination. For EKS deployment, use AWS Load Balancer Controller.

findings.title: Auto-Scaling: No scaling configured
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. AwsAsgUtil.java reads ASG membership of registered services but no auto-scaling is configured for Eureka itself. All capacity is statically provisioned.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling — all capacity statically provisioned. Eureka instances deployed as fixed-size clusters.
findings.recommendation: For EKS deployment, configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) based on registered instance count and request rate. Consider Eureka's peer-awareness protocol during scale-out events.

findings.title: Infrastructure as Code: No IaC exists
findings.description: No infrastructure-as-code exists in this repository. No Terraform files, CloudFormation templates, CDK stacks, Helm charts, or Kustomize manifests were found. All infrastructure is provisioned manually or in a separate repository.
findings.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure created manually (ClickOps) or managed externally. Zero percent codified.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC using AWS CDK (preferred for Java teams) or Terraform to define the full Eureka deployment stack: VPC, subnets, EKS cluster, load balancer, security groups, and DNS records.

findings.title: CI/CD Automation: Build automated but no deployment
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides automated CI (build and test on push/PR) and automated publishing (snapshot/release to Maven Central). No deployment automation exists — no deploy stages, no environment promotion, no rollback.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is manual. No deployment stages, no environment promotion, no automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Extend GitHub Actions with deployment stages: build container image → push to ECR → deploy to staging EKS → run integration tests → promote to production. Use ArgoCD or AWS CodeDeploy.

findings.title: Programming Languages: Compound legacy (Java 8 + Jersey 1 + AWS SDK v1)
findings.description: Java 8 with Jersey 1.19.1 (JAX-RS 1.1), AWS SDK v1 (1.11.277), Servlet 2.5, Guice 4.1.0, and Archaius 0.7.6. Compound legacy across all three axes: language version, framework, and SDK.
findings.gap: Java 8, Jersey 1.x, AWS SDK v1, and Servlet 2.5 are all regressed. Requires version upgrade in addition to framework/SDK modernization.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to Java 17+ (LTS) with Spring Boot 3.x or Jakarta EE 10 replacing Jersey 1.x. Migrate AWS SDK from v1 to v2 for improved performance and non-blocking I/O.

findings.title: Monolith vs Microservices: Modular monolith with clear boundaries
findings.description: Well-structured modular architecture with 10 subprojects having clear separation of concerns. Module boundaries are well-defined with clear interfaces. Dependencies flow server → core → client with no circular dependencies. However, all server modules deploy as a single WAR unit.
findings.gap: Modular monolith — while module boundaries are clear, all server modules deploy as a single WAR artifact. Client and server are properly separated as independent libraries.
findings.recommendation: Current modular structure is appropriate for a service registry. No decomposition needed — Eureka is a cohesive service with single responsibility. Focus on containerization rather than decomposition.

findings.title: Async vs Sync: All communication synchronous HTTP
findings.description: All inter-node communication is synchronous HTTP. Peer replication dispatches events via synchronous Jersey HTTP calls. No async messaging patterns for cross-service state propagation.
findings.gap: All communication synchronous HTTP with no async patterns. For a stateful-crud service propagating state mutations across cluster nodes, this creates tight coupling and cascading failure risk.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge (preferred) or SQS for asynchronous peer notification of registry changes. Synchronous reads (registry fetch) can remain — the gap is in write propagation.

findings.title: Long-Running Process Handling: Mostly async, minor gap
findings.description: Peer replication uses batch processing asynchronously from the request path. Individual API operations are sub-second. Initial registry sync uses configurable retry with waitTimeInMsWhenSyncEmpty.
findings.gap: Most long-running operations handled asynchronously (batch replication doesn't block request path). Minor gap: no formal status polling for cluster convergence status.
findings.recommendation: Consider exposing a cluster convergence status endpoint that consumers can poll to verify registry consistency after scaling events.

findings.title: API Versioning: Limited versioning strategy
findings.description: URL path versioning present (/v2/apps) but only one version exists with no multi-version support, no deprecation strategy, and no versioning documentation.
findings.gap: Versioning applied but limited — only /v2/ prefix exists with no backward compatibility management or version negotiation.
findings.recommendation: Document the API versioning strategy. If a v3 API is planned (e.g., for Jakarta EE migration), implement parallel version support with deprecation timeline. Consider OpenAPI specification.

findings.title: API Authentication: Framework hook without token-based auth
findings.description: ServerRequestAuthFilter is configured in web.xml mapped to all URL patterns, but it is a framework hook/extension point rather than a complete OAuth2/JWT implementation. No token validation visible.
findings.gap: Authentication hook exists but no OAuth2/JWT implementation. Authentication appears to be network-level rather than per-request token-based.
findings.recommendation: Implement per-request authentication using OAuth2/JWT. For EKS, consider API Gateway (preferred) with Cognito authorizer, or integrate Spring Security OAuth2 if migrating to Spring Boot.

findings.title: Centralized Identity: No IdP integration
findings.description: No centralized identity provider integration exists. No Cognito, Okta, OIDC, SAML, or SSO configuration found. Authentication is managed entirely within the application boundary.
findings.gap: Application manages its own authentication entirely with no external IdP integration. No SSO, no federated identity.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito or organizational IdP. For service-to-service authentication, implement IAM-based authentication or mTLS with AWS Private CA.

findings.title: Secrets Management: No managed secrets service
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in source. CI/CD secrets properly stored in GitHub Actions secrets. However, no AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, or rotation mechanism configured for runtime secrets.
findings.gap: No managed secrets service with rotation for runtime credentials. Relies on environment variables and Archaius configuration.
findings.recommendation: Implement AWS Secrets Manager for runtime credentials. Configure automated rotation. Use EKS Secrets Store CSI Driver to mount secrets into pods.

findings.title: Compute Hardening: No patching or scanning
findings.description: No evidence of compute hardening, managed patching, or vulnerability scanning. Dependencies include known-outdated versions (AWS SDK 1.11.277, Jersey 1.19.1, Servlet 2.5).
findings.gap: No patching strategy; no vulnerability scanning. Legacy dependencies have known CVEs not being tracked.
findings.recommendation: Add dependency vulnerability scanning (Dependabot, Snyk, or OWASP Dependency Check) to CI. For deployment, use hardened base images with EKS managed node group auto-updates.

findings.title: Security Pipeline: No security scanning in CI/CD
findings.description: No security scanning tools configured in CI/CD. GitHub Actions runs build only — no SAST, no dependency scanning, no container scanning. No Dependabot, SonarQube, or Semgrep.
findings.gap: No security scanning tools configured. Pipeline has no security validation step. Outdated dependencies are not being flagged.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for automated dependency updates. Integrate SAST (SonarQube or Semgrep) into CI. Add OWASP Dependency Check Gradle plugin.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing: No tracing instrumented
findings.description: No distributed tracing instrumented. No X-Ray, OpenTelemetry, Zipkin, or Jaeger. Netflix Servo provides metrics only, not trace propagation. No trace ID headers propagated.
findings.gap: Cannot trace requests across Eureka peers or from client to server. Debugging replication failures requires manual log correlation.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with AWS X-Ray SDK or OpenTelemetry Java Agent. Propagate trace context across peer replication. For EKS, use AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry (ADOT) sidecar.

findings.title: SLO Definitions: No SLOs defined
findings.description: No SLO definitions found. No availability targets, latency percentile targets, or error budget tracking. Critical gap for a service discovery system where availability impacts all dependent services.
findings.gap: No SLOs — no formal definition of acceptable service levels for the service registry.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs: registry availability (99.99%), registration latency p99 (<100ms), heartbeat processing p99 (<50ms), registry fetch p99 (<200ms). Implement error budget tracking.

findings.title: Business Metrics: Operational metrics exist but not centralized
findings.description: Netflix Servo provides custom operational metrics (registrations, renewals, cancellations, expirations) but these are not published to a centralized monitoring system and no dashboards are configured.
findings.gap: Infrastructure-adjacent operational metrics exist via Servo but not published to CloudWatch or equivalent. No business-outcome metrics (discovery success rate, registry consistency, time-to-discover).
findings.recommendation: Publish Servo metrics to CloudWatch using Micrometer. Define business-meaningful metrics: service discovery success rate, registry consistency percentage, mean time-to-discover.

findings.title: Anomaly Detection: No alerting configured
findings.description: No alerting or anomaly detection configured. No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie. Self-preservation mode is internal but no external alerting when it activates.
findings.gap: No alerting configured. No notification for self-preservation activation, peer replication failure, or registration rate anomalies.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch Alarms on peer replication failure rate, registry size anomaly, self-preservation activation, heartbeat success rate. Integrate with SNS for notifications.

findings.title: Integration Testing: Good coverage with minor gaps
findings.description: Integration tests exist and run in CI. EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest tests client-server REST interactions. MockServer and WireMock used for HTTP integration testing. Tests run as part of gradlew build in CI.
findings.gap: Integration tests exist for primary workflows but gaps remain: no contract tests for REST API, no chaos/fault-injection testing for peer replication, no tests covering AWS-specific paths (EIP, Route53, ASG).
findings.recommendation: Add API contract tests (Pact or Spring Cloud Contract). Add fault-injection tests for peer replication resilience. Consider Testcontainers for testing against real infrastructure.

findings.title: Incident Response: No runbooks or automation
findings.description: No runbooks, incident response automation, or self-healing patterns beyond built-in self-preservation. No SSM Automation, no Lambda remediation, no structured runbooks.
findings.gap: No runbooks — incident response is entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures for common failures.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks (SSM Automation) for: peer node unresponsive, self-preservation triggered, EIP allocation failure, registry divergence. Implement automated remediation.

findings.title: Observability Ownership: No ownership patterns
findings.description: No observability ownership patterns. No CODEOWNERS for observability, no per-service dashboards, no alarms with named owners, no SLO definitions with team attribution.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — monitoring is reactive and fragmented.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS for observability configuration. Create per-service CloudWatch dashboards with team-attributed alarms.

findings.title: Resource Tagging: No tags exist
findings.description: No resource tagging exists because no IaC exists. No default_tags, no tags blocks on resources, no tagging standards.
findings.gap: No tags found on resources; no cost/ownership attribution.
findings.recommendation: When creating IaC, implement consistent tagging from start: Service=eureka, Team=<team>, Environment=<env>, CostCenter=<cc>. Use CDK Aspects or Terraform default_tags.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=3 (meets threshold ≥3); primary trigger not met. Supporting triggers (INF-Q1=1, APP-Q3=1) are met but primary condition APP-Q2<3 is required.

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2=3 meets threshold; pathway requires APP-Q2<3 as primary trigger

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Primary trigger APP-Q2<3 not met (APP-Q2=3). Application has well-defined module boundaries.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 (compute on raw EC2) AND no container definitions found in repository

triggering_questions.note: All compute designed for raw EC2 WAR deployment; no managed compute

detail.current_state: WAR file deployed on EC2 with EIP/ENI/Route53 identity binding
detail.migration_approach: Refactor-then-containerize: abstract EC2-specific identity before containerization

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); no commercial database engines detected in this system

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures or proprietary SQL; criterion met

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. System has no persistent data store.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=Not Evaluated (no persistent data store exists); no database to migrate

triggering_questions.note: Not Evaluated — system has no persistent data store

pathways.not_triggered_reason: System has no persistent data store. No database exists to migrate to managed services.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist; contextual guard prevents trigger
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. Service is a registry, not a data processing system.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (no IaC), INF-Q11=2 (partial CI/CD — build only, no deployment)

triggering_questions.note: No IaC exists — all infrastructure manually provisioned
Build automated but no deployment automation

detail.current_state: GitHub Actions CI for build/test/publish; no IaC; no deployment automation
immediate_actions: Create IaC for target EKS deployment environment
Add deployment stages to CI workflow
Implement integration testing in deployment pipeline

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context is 'Netflix service-discovery server and client' with no AI-related signal terms.
